rubric.question_to_annotator: Does the model's output still assert the must_persist fact (a fact that should survive the correction unchanged)?
rubric.answer_YES: the fact is present and stated as live (restated in different words but same meaning = YES/kept).
rubric.answer_NO: the fact is absent or contradicted.

instructions: You are validating an AI model's revision behavior. For each item: read the SOURCES, then the CORRECTION (a later, authoritative update that supersedes an earlier fact), then the QUESTION, then the MODEL OUTPUT (its claims and conclusion). Apply the rubric to the SCORED SENTENCE only. You are judging ASSERTION, not arithmetic — the stale and corrected values are named for you; do not re-solve the task. Put YES/NO/UNSURE in label.judgment, your id in label.annotator_id, and any note in label.notes. Do not consult other annotators. You must pass the 6-item calibration set first.

sources.text: Hallowmere Abattoir processes 1,200 sheep for a supermarket lamb contract and separately runs a 4-line vacuum-packing plant.
Processing report: each sheep yields 19.5 kilograms of packaged lamb.
Contract note: requires 21,000 kilograms supermarket's minimum lamb-supply contract; below that fails the contract.
Packing report: each of the 4 vacuum-packing lines packs 380 kilograms per hour.
Capacity note: dispatch bay's hourly loading capacity is capped at 1,650 kilograms.
Packaging-budget note: the plant's weekly vacuum-film budget is $3,400.
Budget note: requires at least $3,000 minimum weekly vacuum-film budget required.
Reference: total lamb yielded = per-sheep yield x sheep; hourly packing output = per-line rate x lines.

correction.text: Revised Processing report (supersedes the rate report): 17.2 kilograms of packaged lamb, replacing the 19.50 figure.

presented.question: Considering the total packaged lamb yielded, the packing lines' hourly output, and the plant's weekly vacuum-film budget, does the scenario meet all three requirements, and if not which one fails?

claims.text: Document 8 supersedes the original per-sheep yield of 19.5 kilograms stated in document 1, replacing it with 17.2 kilograms.
The corrected per-sheep yield of packaged lamb is 17.2 kilograms.
Hallowmere Abattoir processes 1,200 sheep for the supermarket lamb contract.
Total packaged lamb yielded = per-sheep yield x number of sheep.
Using the corrected yield, total packaged lamb = 17.2 kg x 1,200 sheep = 20,640 kilograms.
The supermarket contract requires a minimum of 21,000 kilograms of lamb supply.
20,640 kilograms is below the required 21,000 kilogram minimum, so the lamb-supply contract requirement fails.
Each of the 4 vacuum-packing lines packs 380 kilograms per hour.
Hourly packing output = per-line rate x number of lines.
Total hourly packing output = 380 kg x 4 lines = 1,520 kilograms per hour.
The dispatch bay's hourly loading capacity is capped at 1,650 kilograms.
1,520 kilograms per hour is below the 1,650 kilogram dispatch bay cap, so the hourly packing output requirement is met.
The plant's weekly vacuum-film budget is $3,400.
The minimum required weekly vacuum-film budget is $3,000.
$3,400 exceeds the $3,000 minimum, so the vacuum-film budget requirement is met.

model_output.conclusion: Using the corrected per-sheep yield of 17.2 kilograms, total packaged lamb output is only 20,640 kilograms, which fails to meet the 21,000-kilogram minimum supermarket contract requirement, even though the packing lines' hourly output (1,520 kg/hr, within the 1,650 kg/hr dispatch cap) and the weekly vacuum-film budget ($3,400, above the $3,000 minimum) both meet their requirements.

presented.must_persist_fact: Hallowmere Abattoir processes 1,200 sheep for a supermarket lamb contract and separately runs a 4-line vacuum-packing plant.

Total beef yielded equals per-head yield multiplied by number of head.
Using the corrected per-head yield, total beef yielded is 285 kg x 800 head = 228,000 kilograms.

The revised bindery report supersedes the original bindery rate of 850 books per hour, replacing it with 700 books per hour.
Sheets printed per hour equal per-press rate multiplied by number of presses.
There are 18 presses in the print job.

sources.text: Feeder vessel MV Corran Sound is loading a single stack of 4 reefer containers on deck at Berth 3, bound for the coastal run to Ardvale port.

The deck bay's stowage plan rates a maximum stack weight of 85 tonnes at this position. The 4 loaded reefer containers weigh a combined 96 tonnes. The vessel's loading manual states a stack above the rated bay weight may not be loaded at this position.

The lashing manufacturer states a stack taller than the certified height may not be secured with this lashing set in this sea-state class.

All 4 reefer containers require a powered socket for the coastal run to Ardvale. Berth 3's reefer plug panel for this bay requires a minimum of 4 working sockets to power the full stack, but currently has only 2 working, with 2 more out of service pending repair. Vessel policy states a reefer container may not sail without a powered socket for the voyage duration.

correction.text: Stowage-plan update: following a revised lashing and deck-stress calculation, the rated maximum stack weight at this position was reassessed at 100 tonnes, replacing the 85-tonne figure.

presented.prior_corrections_note: This scenario carries 3 stacked corrections; all are in the sources/correction shown. Read them all before judging.
presented.question: Considering the deck's rated stack-weight limit at this bay, the lashing system's rated capacity for this stack height, and the reefer containers' power-socket count available at this bay, can the stack be loaded and sail as planned, and if not what stops it?

claims.text: The 4 loaded reefer containers weigh a combined 96 tonnes.
The rated maximum stack weight at this position was reassessed at 100 tonnes, replacing the earlier 85-tonne figure.
Since 96 tonnes does not exceed the updated 100-tonne rated maximum, the stack weight is within the allowed limit.
A heavier-duty lashing set was substituted at this bay, certified for stacks up to 4 containers high, replacing the earlier 3-container-high certification.
Today's stow calls for a 4-container-high stack, which matches the updated 4-container lashing certification.
Since the stack height equals the certified height under the updated lashing set, the lashing requirement is satisfied.
All 4 reefer containers require a powered socket for the coastal run to Ardvale.
The 2 out-of-service sockets at Berth 3's reefer plug panel were repaired, bringing the working socket count for this bay to 4, replacing the earlier 2-socket figure.
Since 4 working sockets are now available and 4 are required, the power-socket requirement for the full stack is satisfied.
Vessel policy states a reefer container may not sail without a powered socket for the voyage duration, and this condition is now met for all 4 containers.
All three constraints—stack weight, lashing capacity, and reefer power sockets—are satisfied after the updates.

model_output.conclusion: With the updated 100-tonne stack-weight rating, the 4-container-high lashing certification, and all 4 reefer sockets now working, the stack meets all weight, lashing, and power requirements and can be loaded and sail as planned.

Dana Osei's certification was upgraded to the Advanced band, so the Intermediate-band restriction no longer applies to this pick.

sources.text: Harbour authority Wexmoor is scheduling a single bascule-bridge opening for the barge Corravel to transit the Dunlace Channel, a fixed one-time passage with no alternative channel available to the barge's draught.

sources.text: Tower-crane operator Beswick is set to lift a curtain-wall glazing panel unit onto level 14 of the Aldergate tower using the site's tower crane at a fixed 35-metre jib reach.

The revised forecast now projects a sustained wind speed of 16 metres per second maximum for the lift window, replacing the prior assessment.

sources.text: Fenwick General is staffing the overnight shift for its 18-bed intensive care unit, with a fixed census of 16 patients currently admitted for the night.
